Container.Name: hermes
Container.Overview: A Telegram bot that lets your users report media issues to Seerr (the unified Overseerr/Jellyseerr successor), with optional self-service auto-fix that tells Radarr or Sonarr to delete the file and re-search. All configuration happens in a built-in admin web UI -- no environment variables required.
Container.Changes:
### v0.8.0 - All settings moved to the admin web UI. No environment variables required to install -- start the container, open the web UI, finish setup.
### v0.7.0 - Slim admin web UI at /admin (settings, backup/restore, password change)
### v0.6.0 - Comment-reply notifications via Seerr webhook
Container.Description:
A Telegram bot that lets your users report media issues to [Seerr](https://seerr.dev/), with optional self-service auto-fix.
**Setup** 1. Install the container (no environment variables needed) 2. Click the **WebUI** button on the container card (or open `http://<unraid-ip>:8765/admin`) 3. The first-run page collects: admin username + password, Telegram bot token (from @BotFather), your Telegram user ID (DM @userinfobot to get yours), and Seerr URL + API key 4. Save -- the container restarts and the bot comes online 5. Log in and configure Radarr/Sonarr, auto-fix allowlist, webhook secret, etc. 6. In Telegram, DM `/setprivacy` to @BotFather, select your bot, choose **Disable** (lets the bot see commands in group chats) 7. DM the bot `/link` to authorize with Plex
**Web UI features** - Edit any setting with hot reload (Telegram bot token + admin ID changes restart the container automatically) - Download an encrypted backup ZIP (settings + mappings DB + encryption key) - Restore from backup (container restarts automatically) - Change admin password
**Enable comment-reply notifications (optional)** 1. In Seerr, go to Settings → Notifications → Webhook 2. Webhook URL: `http://<unraid-ip>:8765/webhook/seerr` 3. Authorization Header: paste the Webhook Secret from the Hermes web UI (leave blank if no secret) 4. Enable the **Issue Comment** notification type 5. Click "Test" to confirm Hermes receives it
See the [project page](https://github.com/cajunflavoredbob/hermes) for full docs.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: Stores settings.json, user mappings DB, encryption key, session secret.

Config.Name: Web UI / Webhook Port
Config.Description: Port for both the admin web UI (/admin) and Seerr webhook receiver (/webhook/seerr).

Config.Name: Encryption Key (advanced)
Config.Description: Fernet key used to encrypt stored Plex tokens at rest. Leave blank for normal use -- Hermes generates one on first run and persists it to /data/encryption.key. Set this only if you need to control the key explicitly (e.g. migrating from another install). Generate with: python -c "from cryptography.fernet import Fernet; print(Fernet.generate_key().decode())"

Config.Name: Trusted Proxies (advanced)
Config.Description: Comma-separated IPs/CIDRs of reverse proxies allowed to set X-Forwarded-For / X-Forwarded-Proto (e.g. your Nginx Proxy Manager address). Leave blank when the admin UI is accessed directly -- forwarded headers are then ignored because clients can forge them. See 'Securing the admin UI' in the README.
